title: CSRF Protection Bypass on Sensitive Endpoint

steps.message: Analyze Splunk logs for the '/api/v1/user/update_email' endpoint to identify requests with missing or invalid anti-CSRF tokens and correlate with user session IDs.

Implement cryptographically secure, per-session anti-CSRF tokens for the vulnerable endpoint. Ensure tokens are validated server-side and not transmitted via URL query strings to prevent CSS-based enumeration or Referer leakage.
Apply UI Redress protection by implementing 'X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN' or a Content Security Policy (CSP) with 'frame-ancestors' to prevent clickjacking attacks that bypass CSRF defenses.
Perform a scan for Reflected and Stored XSS vulnerabilities on the application. Per the KB, XSS can be leveraged to read anti-CSRF tokens and execute requests on behalf of users.
Review and approve the updated session handling rules and token validation logic before deployment to production.
Notify executive leadership of the vulnerability remediation on the high-risk email update endpoint.
